step.title: Readiness Gate
step.goal: Evaluate whether the Q↔R loop has produced enough understanding to exit to the Design phase. If all five readiness criteria from QRDSPIV are met, summarize and hand off to ticket-design. If any is not met, loop back to step 2 for another turn.

substep.title: Evaluate the Five Criteria
substep.action:
Check each criterion against the accumulated bundle state. Per QRDSPIV §Q↔R Readiness Criteria and spec §3.1:
1. **The problem is clearly scoped (user confirmed)** - Restatement in the ticket body matches what was grilled - Scope boundaries are explicit (in-scope AND out-of-scope clear) - User has confirmed scope, not just acknowledged it
2. **The current state is factually mapped (codebase, artifacts)** - Research file has factual findings for every relevant code area - No "we don't know if X exists" gaps remain on critical questions - Subagent findings have been synthesized, not just collected
3. **Impact is understood (what artifacts need creating/modifying)** - The downstream artifacts implied by this ticket are identified at a high level (e.g., "this needs an ADR for the data isolation pattern; a spec for team APIs; an implementation across N vertical slices") - Risk areas are surfaced (e.g., "auth middleware refactor will touch 47 test files")
4. **Edge cases are identified (deletion, error states, permissions)** - Edge cases relevant to the primary tag are enumerated and have chosen answers - "What happens when X is deleted?" / "What happens on permission denial?" / "What's the concurrency story?" — at least asked, ideally answered
5. **The agent can articulate what it knows WITHOUT being asked** - Most rigorous criterion. The agent should be able to walk through the scope, current state, design implications, risks, and edge cases from the bundle alone, without re-reading the user's original message.
For each criterion, record: - PASS / NOT YET - One-line evidence (e.g., "ticket body lines 12-25 cover scope; out-of-scope listed in lines 30-35")
Store as {{session.readiness_check}}.

check.action: The loop is ready to exit. Proceed to substep 6c.
The loop continues. Proceed to substep 6d to surface the gap and loop back to step 2.

substep.title: Confirm Exit With User

check.action: Capture the gap into session state as the next turn's focus. Loop back to step 2.

The bundle on disk is the handoff state. Acknowledge and exit the skill without writing a handoff section to the questions file beyond what step 5 already wrote. The user can resume by re-invoking ticket-grill on the same bundle path.

substep.title: Surface the Gap and Loop

check.action: Capture the next turn's focus into session state. Loop back to step 2.
Note the force-exit in the questions file (which criteria were unmet at exit). The bundle is technically usable but flagged as under-grilled. Proceed to substep 6e.
Exit the skill. Bundle on disk is the handoff state.

substep.title: Write Readiness Summary to Worklog
Present Final Handoff

section.title: The fifth criterion is the strict one
section.description: Criteria 1-4 are objective checks against the bundle's content. Criterion 5 — "the agent can articulate what it knows WITHOUT being asked" — is the rigorous one. If the agent can only articulate the work by re-reading the user's original request or by paraphrasing prior Q&A, the loop is not actually done. The articulation should flow from the bundle's accumulated state.

section.title: Force-exit is a defined failure mode

rules.rule: Force-exit produces an under-grilled ticket. ticket-design or ticket-verify will surface the gaps later, but at higher cost
Force-exit should be RARE — reserved for cases where the user has external time pressure
The questions file MUST record which criteria were unmet at force-exit; ticket-verify will check this
Do not encourage force-exit; offer it as an explicit option but recommend against it

section.title: Loop-back is normal
section.description: Spec §3.2 lists backward loops as permitted. ticket-design may return to ticket-grill if D-phase alignment reveals a scoping gap. That re-entry is handled by step 1 (load bundle) detecting prior Q&A entries and resuming from the readiness gate's last failure state. No special "resume mode" is needed.

section.title: Three-strike escalation is a separate concern
section.description: Per Lock 31, when the user reverses a foundational decision after D-phase alignment, a three-strike pattern escalates to abort recommendation. That pattern is OWNED BY ticket-design (the D-phase skill), not here — ticket-grill simply absorbs the new focus and loops. The strike counter sits in ticket-design's session state.
